mechanism_narrative: A low and stable unemployment rate indicates a healthy labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate profits, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest UNRATE value is 13.3, which is high, but the mechanism describes conditions for positive returns under low UNRATE.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are consistently negative or flat during periods where UNRATE is below 5% for 6 consecutive months, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is at an extremely low level, it signals an accommodative monetary policy, encouraging risk-taking and investment in equities, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest FEDFUNDS value is 0.05, indicating an extremely low rate environment.
regime_hint: zero lower bound or near-zero interest rate environment
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% over any 5-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and housing starts indicates weakening consumer demand and investment, signaling a broader economic slowdown that negatively impacts corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1% and HOUST declines > 5% over 3 months
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances where both RSAFS and HOUST show significant declines, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, an increasing industrial production index signifies a rebound in manufacturing and supply-side activity, leading to improved corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases > 0.5% month-over-month for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: If INDPRO increases as specified during a post-recession recovery, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are consistently negative or flat, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in GDP indicates a robust economy with strong aggregate demand, which typically translates into higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 shows consistent quarter-over-quarter growth (e.g., > 1% annualized) but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are predominantly negative over any 5-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, leading to expectations of tighter monetary policy and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.5% for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL accelerates as specified, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are consistently positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong and consistent job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls, indicates a healthy labor market and robust consumer confidence, supporting equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: economic expansion with strong job growth
failure_condition: If PAYEMS consistently increases by more than 100,000 for three months, but S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are predominantly negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising unemployment and a low federal funds rate suggests a central bank responding to economic weakness, which can signal a bottoming process and eventual recovery, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 1.0pp over 3 months AND FEDFUNDS < 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:Latest UNRATE is 13.3, FEDFUNDS is 0.05. This snapshot fits the trigger condition for UNRATE rising and FEDFUNDS being low.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises significantly while FEDFUNDS remains low, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are consistently negative for more than 6 months following the trigger, the hypothesis is rejected.
